probes.name: lighting_automation_routine
probes.prompt: Please set up an evening wind-down routine: at 21:00 dim the three lights above my desk to 20% and leave the main one at full, then at 22:00 turn off all lights gently over 5 minutes. I want this to repeat every day.
probes.criteria:
Agent is RALF planning stage. A good response breaks this into 3-7 concrete stages covering: (1) identify and confirm the light entities involved, (2) create the 21:00 dimming automation with correct brightness values, (3) create the 22:00 gradual off automation with transition time, (4) schedule both as daily repeating routines, (5) verify/confirm the plan before spawning evaluation. Each stage must have an observable finalization criterion (e.g. 'automation entity X created and visible in Home Assistant').
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 16 and 25 about dimming lights and turning them off gently.

probes.name: thesis_defense_prep_plan
probes.prompt: I need to come up with two questions I think the supervisor will ask at the defense and prepare solid answers for them. I'm defending a thesis about microservice architecture patterns. Help me plan this out step by step.
probes.criteria:
Agent is RALF planning stage. A good response creates stages such as: (1) research common thesis defense questions for CS/software engineering, (2) generate 4-6 candidate questions specific to microservice architecture, (3) help narrow to the 2 most likely questions, (4) draft structured answers with key points, (5) review and refine answers. Each stage needs a clear deliverable and finalization criterion (e.g. 'list of 6 candidate questions produced', 'final 2 answers approved by user').
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: medication_tracking_day_plan
probes.prompt: Plan out how to track my meds today: I've already taken 5mg of mph this morning. I'll probably take another 5mg around noon, and atenza 36mg in the evening. Log what I've taken and set up reminders for the remaining doses.
probes.criteria:
Agent is RALF planning stage. A good response breaks this into concrete stages: (1) log the already-taken 5mg mph dose with timestamp, (2) create a noon reminder for the second 5mg mph dose, (3) create an evening reminder for atenza 36mg, (4) set up confirmation/logging prompts after each reminder fires. Each stage must have observable finalization criteria like 'reminder entity created and visible' or 'dose logged in tracking system'.

probes.name: video_production_pipeline
probes.prompt: I need to produce a short video about git worktrees for my YouTube channel. Plan out the full pipeline: research the topic, write a script with voiceover, generate visuals, record narration, and assemble the final cut. The target length is about 8 minutes.
probes.criteria:
Agent is RALF planning stage. A good response creates 5-10 stages covering: (1) research git worktrees and outline key points, (2) write a full script with narration segments, (3) plan/generate visual assets or code demonstrations, (4) produce voiceover audio, (5) assemble video with synchronized audio and visuals, (6) final review and export. Each stage must have observable criteria (e.g. 'script document contains all sections with word count', 'audio file exported at correct length').

probes.name: shopping_and_meal_logging
probes.prompt: I'm going to buy groceries now. Here's what I need: cream, bread, cottage cheese, tuna, rice, and some vegetables. After shopping, I want to log everything I ate today: cottage cheese with bread for breakfast, some KFC chicken around lunch, and I'm planning rice with tuna salad for dinner. Build me a plan to handle the shopping and food logging.
probes.criteria:
Agent is RALF planning stage. A good response separates into distinct stages: (1) retrieve or create shopping list with specified items, (2) log breakfast (cottage cheese + bread) with estimated macros, (3) log lunch (KFC chicken) with estimated macros, (4) plan/pre-log dinner (rice + tuna salad), (5) after shopping confirm items purchased and update inventory. Each stage needs a clear finalization criterion like 'shopping list shows 6 items checked off' or '3 meals logged in nutrition tracker with macro totals'.
